title: DeepSeek-R1-distill abliterated (community)
description: Refusal-direction-ablated variant of DeepSeek-R1-distill-Llama-8B (and 70B). Run via Ollama or LM Studio, point the local-provider model field at the HF id below.

payload.notes: Community-maintained. Confirm hashes against the upstream HuggingFace repo before deploying.

title: Qwen3 abliterated (community)
description: Refusal-direction-ablated Qwen3 family. Multiple sizes; pick the variant your hardware supports.

payload.notes: Various size variants exist; check the huihui-ai or mlabonne HF org for the latest.

title: Llama-3.1-8B abliterated (mlabonne)
description: The original abliteration recipe author's own Llama-3.1-8B variant. Reference implementation; useful for comparing community abliterations against the baseline technique.

payload.notes: Authored by Maxime Labonne, who popularized the abliteration technique in June 2024.

title: Llama-3.2-3B abliterated (community)
description: Smaller Llama-3.2-3B abliterated variant. Fits on consumer hardware (8GB VRAM); useful for fast local iteration without a server-class GPU.

payload.notes: 3B parameter variant. Quality is lower than 8B but runs on a laptop GPU.

title: Mistral-7B Uncensored (community fine-tune)
description: Not strict abliteration; a community fine-tune that strips most refusal behaviors. Useful baseline for testing what 'uncensored' means without the weight-edit technique.

payload.notes: Eric Hartford's Dolphin fine-tune series. Fine-tuned, not abliterated, but behavioral results similar.

title: Gemma-2-9B abliterated (community)
description: Refusal-direction-ablated Gemma-2-9B-it. Useful for testing whether Cryptex attack techniques transfer to Google base models when their alignment layer is removed.

payload.notes: Gemma-2-9B-it abliteration. Compare against vanilla Gemma-2-9B-it to isolate alignment-layer behaviors from base-model capabilities.

title: Qwen2.5-Coder abliterated (for code-generation research)
description: Abliterated coder variant. Used for evaluating code-side guardrails (Should the model refuse to write certain code patterns?).

payload.notes: Coder-specialized. Useful for testing whether code-side safety training survives abliteration.

title: Llama-3.3-70B abliterated (server-class)
description: Largest Llama-3.3-70B abliterated variant. Requires server-class GPU (A100/H100 or 2x4090); too heavy for most laptops but high-quality output.

payload.notes: 70B parameters. Run via vLLM or llama.cpp Q4_K_M GGUF on 2x4090. Avoid full FP16 on consumer hardware.

title: DeepSeek-V3 abliterated (community)
description: Refusal-ablated DeepSeek-V3-Lite (MoE family). Smaller than full DeepSeek-V3 but inherits much of its capability ceiling.

payload.notes: MoE architecture. Memory profile is large despite the Lite designation.

title: Phi-3-mini abliterated (consumer hardware)
description: Microsoft Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct refusal-ablated. 3.8B params; fits on a laptop. Useful for testing whether Phi family alignment training survives abliteration.

payload.notes: Smallest Phi-3 variant. Quality below Llama-3.x but extremely fast inference.

title: Qwen3-8B abliterated v2 (huihui-ai)
description: v2 release with corrected layer-0 abliteration that fixes the garbled-output regression seen in v1. First-class thinking / nothink toggle inherits from Qwen3 — directly addresses the reasoning gap in the older Qwen3-7B vault entry.

payload.notes: 8B BF16 ~16GB. Laptop / desktop class. Thinking-toggle supported.

title: Qwen3-4B abliterated v2 (laptop anchor)
description: Smallest competent thinking-capable abliterated Qwen3. Laptop-class anchor (4B, ~8GB). Fixed-layer-0 v2 release.

payload.notes: 4B params. Fits on a 12GB laptop GPU comfortably. Thinking-toggle supported.

title: DeepSeek-R1-0528 Qwen3-8B abliterated
description: May 2025 DeepSeek-R1 refresh distilled into Qwen3-8B. Native long-form reasoning trace, refusal-ablated. Stronger than the older R1-Distill-Llama-8B vault entry for refusal-resistant chain-of-thought research.

payload.notes: Latest DeepSeek-R1 distillation generation. Visible <think> CoT channel.

title: QwQ-32B abliterated (long-form reasoner)
description: Alibaba's pre-Qwen3 deliberative reasoning model, refusal-ablated. 17+ downstream community merges built on top of this base. Server-class (33B).

payload.notes: 33B params. ~64GB BF16; ~20GB at 4-bit. Long-form deliberation reasoner.

title: Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B abliterated (MoE)
description: Abliterated coder MoE: 31B total parameters, only 3B active. Desktop GPUs can run via offloading. Major capability jump over the earlier Qwen2.5-Coder-7B entry for code-side guardrail research.

payload.notes: MoE 31B/3B-active. Coder-specialized abliterated variant.

title: Qwen2.5-VL-7B abliterated (vision-language)
description: First vision-language abliterated model in the vault. Text-pathway refusal-ablated; vision tower untouched. Useful for image-conditioned jailbreak research.

payload.notes: Vision-language. Laptop / desktop class.

title: Qwen3-VL-8B abliterated (newer-gen vision)
description: Newer Qwen3-generation vision-language abliterated variant. Pair with the Qwen2.5-VL entry to compare generation-level VL behavior under refusal-direction ablation.

payload.notes: Vision-language. ~50k monthly downloads at vault-update time.

title: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B abliterated (mid-gen MoE)
description: Mid-generation Qwen3.5 MoE. Ships its own thinking template. Strong research-grade reasoner slot above 30B but below 70B.

payload.notes: MoE 36B/3B-active. Thinking-mode native.

title: Qwen3.6-27B-MTP abliterated (latest gen)
description: Latest Qwen3.6 generation (May 2026). MTP = multi-token prediction variant. Newest dense Qwen abliterated when this vault entry shipped.

payload.notes: 27B dense. Multi-token-prediction speedup variant.

title: Gemma-4-26B-A4B abliterated (any-to-any multimodal)
description: Newest Google Gemma generation, refusal-ablated. Any-to-any multimodal. ~74k monthly downloads — most-downloaded entry in the huihui catalogue at the time of vault refresh. Supersedes the older Gemma-2-9B entry.

payload.notes: Any-to-any multimodal. 27B class. Desktop GPU comfortable.

title: Granite-4.1-30B abliterated (IBM enterprise base)
description: First non-Qwen / Llama / Gemma / Mistral base family in the vault. Granite-4.1 has a distinct safety training pipeline; useful for diversifying refusal-direction research surface.

payload.notes: 29B dense. IBM enterprise base — different alignment lineage than the Llama / Qwen / Gemma trees.

title: Phi-4 abliterated (MIT-licensed Microsoft)
description: Phi-4 refusal-ablated. MIT-licensed; much more capable successor to the older Phi-3-mini entry (15B vs 3.8B, current-gen training data).

payload.notes: 15B dense. Strong code + math benchmark surface.

title: gpt-oss-20b abliterated (OpenAI-lineage)
description: The only OpenAI-lineage weights in the vault. Major distribution-shift target for red-team comparisons since RLHF style differs from Meta / Alibaba / Google trees.

payload.notes: 21B BF16. OpenAI-style alignment pipeline; useful baseline for cross-lineage refusal research.

title: Mistral-Small-24B-2501 abliterated
description: Mistral-Small-24B (January 2025 release), refusal-ablated. Only abliterated Mistral above 7B in the vault.

payload.notes: 24B dense. Featherless-AI inference-provider available out of the box.
